verses.greek: Παῦλος κλητὸς ἀπόστολος ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ καὶ Σωσθένης ὁ ἀδελφὸς
verses.mistral: Paul, called as an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Sosthenes, his brother.
verses.kjv: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,

verses.greek: τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ, ⸂ἡγιασμένοις ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ, τῇ οὔσῃ ἐν Κορίνθῳ,⸃ κλητοῖς ἁγίοις, σὺν πᾶσιν τοῖς ἐπικαλουμένοις τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ ἐν παντὶ τόπῳ ⸀αὐτῶν καὶ ἡμῶν·
verses.mistral: To the church of God which is in Corinth, to those sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, together with all those who in every place call upon the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, both theirs and ours:
verses.kjv: Unto the church of God which is at Corinth, to them that are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, with all that in every place call upon the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both their's and our's:

words.gloss: I call by an additional name, appeal to

verses.greek: χάρις ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν καὶ κυρίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: Εὐχαριστῶ τῷ θεῷ ⸀μου πάντοτε περὶ ὑμῶν ἐπὶ τῇ χάριτι τοῦ θεοῦ τῇ δοθείσῃ ὑμῖν ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ,
verses.mistral: Thank you to God for you always, through His grace given to you in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: I thank my God always on your behalf, for the grace of God which is given you by Jesus Christ;

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐν παντὶ ἐπλουτίσθητε ἐν αὐτῷ, ἐν παντὶ λόγῳ καὶ πάσῃ γνώσει,
verses.mistral: In Him you have been enriched in every way, in all speech and knowledge.
verses.kjv: That in every thing ye are enriched by him, in all utterance, and in all knowledge;

verses.greek: καθὼς τὸ μαρτύριον τοῦ Χριστοῦ ἐβεβαιώθη ἐν ὑμῖν,
verses.mistral: Was established in you, as the testimony of Christ.
verses.kjv: Even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you:

verses.greek: ὥστε ὑμᾶς μὴ ὑστερεῖσθαι ἐν μηδενὶ χαρίσματι, ἀπεκδεχομένους τὴν ἀποκάλυψιν τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ·
verses.mistral: You should not be left behind in any gift, awaiting the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: So that ye come behind in no gift; waiting for the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ:

words.gloss: I am lacking, fall short, suffer need
I expect eagerly, wait for eagerly

verses.greek: ὃς καὶ βεβαιώσει ὑμᾶς ἕως τέλους ἀνεγκλήτους ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: He will strengthen you to the end, so that you may be blameless on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: Who shall also confirm you unto the end, that ye may be blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: πιστὸς ὁ θεὸς δι’ οὗ ἐκλήθητε εἰς κοινωνίαν τοῦ υἱοῦ αὐτοῦ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν.
verses.mistral: Faithful is God, through whom you were called into fellowship with His Son Jesus Christ our Lord.
verses.kjv: God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.

verses.greek: Παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς, ἀδελφοί, διὰ τοῦ ὀνόματος τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ ἵνα τὸ αὐτὸ λέγητε πάντες, καὶ μὴ ᾖ ἐν ὑμῖν σχίσματα, ἦτε δὲ κατηρτισμένοι ἐν τῷ αὐτῷ νοῒ καὶ ἐν τῇ αὐτῇ γνώμῃ.
verses.mistral: I entreat you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you; but that ye be perfected together in the same mind and in the same judgment.
verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you; but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same mind and in the same judgment.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐδηλώθη γάρ μοι περὶ ὑμῶν, ἀδελφοί μου, ὑπὸ τῶν Χλόης ὅτι ἔριδες ἐν ὑμῖν εἰσιν.
verses.mistral: Strife exists among you, brethren.
verses.kjv: For it hath been declared unto me of you, my brethren, by them which are of the house of Chloe, that there are contentions among you.

verses.greek: λέγω δὲ τοῦτο ὅτι ἕκαστος ὑμῶν λέγει· Ἐγὼ μέν εἰμι Παύλου, Ἐγὼ δὲ Ἀπολλῶ, Ἐγὼ δὲ Κηφᾶ, Ἐγὼ δὲ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Each of you says, "I am of Paul," or "I am of Apollos," or "I am of Cephas," or "I am of Christ."
verses.kjv: Now this I say, that every one of you saith, I am of Paul; and I of Apollos; and I of Cephas; and I of Christ.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: μεμέρισται ὁ Χριστός; μὴ Παῦλος ἐσταυρώθη ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν, ἢ εἰς τὸ ὄνομα Παύλου ἐβαπτίσθητε;
verses.mistral: Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you, or were you baptized in the name of Paul?
verses.kjv: Is Christ divided? was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?

verses.greek: ⸀εὐχαριστῶ ὅτι οὐδένα ὑμῶν ἐβάπτισα εἰ μὴ Κρίσπον καὶ Γάϊον,
verses.mistral: I thank God that none of you were baptized in my name, except Crispus and Gaius.
verses.kjv: I thank God that I baptized none of you, but Crispus and Gaius;

verses.greek: ἵνα μή τις εἴπῃ ὅτι εἰς τὸ ἐμὸν ὄνομα ⸀ἐβαπτίσθητε·
verses.mistral: Nobody should say that they were baptized in my name.
verses.kjv: Lest any should say that I had baptized in mine own name.

verses.greek: ἐβάπτισα δὲ καὶ τὸν Στεφανᾶ οἶκον· λοιπὸν οὐκ οἶδα εἴ τινα ἄλλον ἐβάπτισα.
verses.mistral: I also baptized the household of Stephanas. Beyond that, I do not know whether I baptized anyone else.
verses.kjv: And I baptized also the household of Stephanas: besides, I know not whether I baptized any other.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ἀπέστειλέν με Χριστὸς βαπτίζειν ἀλλὰ εὐαγγελίζεσθαι, οὐκ ἐν σοφίᾳ λόγου, ἵνα μὴ κενωθῇ ὁ σταυρὸς τοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Christ did not send me to baptize, but to preach the gospel, not with eloquent wisdom, lest the cross of Christ be emptied of its power.
verses.kjv: For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the gospel: not with wisdom of words, lest the cross of Christ should be made of none effect.

words.gloss: I send forth, send, send away, dismiss
I bring good news, preach good tidings
I empty, deprive of content, make unreal

verses.greek: Ὁ λόγος γὰρ ὁ τοῦ σταυροῦ τοῖς μὲν ἀπολλυμένοις μωρία ἐστίν, τοῖς δὲ σῳζομένοις ἡμῖν δύναμις θεοῦ ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: The word of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing, but to us who are being saved, it is the power of God.
verses.kjv: For the preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the power of God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: γέγραπται γάρ· Ἀπολῶ τὴν σοφίαν τῶν σοφῶν, καὶ τὴν σύνεσιν τῶν συνετῶν ἀθετήσω.
verses.mistral: Destroys the wisdom of the wise, and the intelligence of the intelligent.
verses.kjv: For it is written, I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and will bring to nothing the understanding of the prudent.

verses.greek: ποῦ σοφός; ποῦ γραμματεύς; ποῦ συζητητὴς τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου; οὐχὶ ἐμώρανεν ὁ θεὸς τὴν σοφίαν τοῦ ⸀κόσμου;
verses.mistral: Where is the wise? Where is the scribe? Where is the debater of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
verses.kjv: Where is the wise? where is the scribe? where is the disputer of this world? hath not God made foolish the wisdom of this world?

words.gloss: by no means, not at all
I make foolish, taint, make useless

verses.greek: ἐπειδὴ γὰρ ἐν τῇ σοφίᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ οὐκ ἔγνω ὁ κόσμος διὰ τῆς σοφίας τὸν θεόν, εὐδόκησεν ὁ θεὸς διὰ τῆς μωρίας τοῦ κηρύγματος σῶσαι τοὺς πιστεύοντας.
verses.mistral: In the wisdom of God, the world did not know Him through the wisdom, so God was well-pleased through the foolishness of the message to save those who believe.
verses.kjv: For after that in the wisdom of God the world by wisdom knew not God, it pleased God by the foolishness of preaching to save them that believe.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: ἐπειδὴ καὶ Ἰουδαῖοι ⸀σημεῖα αἰτοῦσιν καὶ Ἕλληνες σοφίαν ζητοῦσιν·
verses.mistral: Jews demand signs, Greeks seek wisdom.
verses.kjv: For the Jews require a sign, and the Greeks seek after wisdom:

verses.greek: ἡμεῖς δὲ κηρύσσομεν Χριστὸν ἐσταυρωμένον, Ἰουδαίοις μὲν σκάνδαλον ⸀ἔθνεσιν δὲ μωρίαν,
verses.mistral: We preach Christ crucified, to the Jews a stumbling block and to the Greeks foolishness.
verses.kjv: But we preach Christ crucified, unto the Jews a stumblingblock, and unto the Greeks foolishness;

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: αὐτοῖς δὲ τοῖς κλητοῖς, Ἰουδαίοις τε καὶ Ἕλλησιν, Χριστὸν θεοῦ δύναμιν καὶ θεοῦ σοφίαν.
verses.mistral: To them that are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God, and the wisdom of God.
verses.kjv: But unto them which are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the power of God, and the wisdom of God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὅτι τὸ μωρὸν τοῦ θεοῦ σοφώτερον τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἐστίν, καὶ τὸ ἀσθενὲς τοῦ θεοῦ ἰσχυρότερον τῶν ⸀ἀνθρώπων.
verses.mistral: Wise is the foolishness of God to man, and powerful is God's weakness to man.
verses.kjv: Because the foolishness of God is wiser than men; and the weakness of God is stronger than men.

verses.greek: Βλέπετε γὰρ τὴν κλῆσιν ὑμῶν, ἀδελφοί, ὅτι οὐ πολλοὶ σοφοὶ κατὰ σάρκα, οὐ πολλοὶ δυνατοί, οὐ πολλοὶ εὐγενεῖς·
verses.mistral: You behold that not many wise according to flesh, not many powerful, not many noble are called
verses.kjv: For ye see your calling, brethren, how that not many wise men after the flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are called:

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ τὰ μωρὰ τοῦ κόσμου ἐξελέξατο ὁ θεός, ἵνα ⸂καταισχύνῃ τοὺς σοφούς⸃, καὶ τὰ ἀσθενῆ τοῦ κόσμου ἐξελέξατο ὁ θεός, ἵνα καταισχύνῃ τὰ ἰσχυρά,
verses.mistral: But God chose the foolish things of the world, to shame the wise; and God chose the weak things of the world, to shame the strong.
verses.kjv: But God hath chosen the foolish things of the world to confound the wise; and God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty;

words.gloss: I shame, disgrace, put to utter confusion
I shame, disgrace, put to utter confusion

verses.greek: καὶ τὰ ἀγενῆ τοῦ κόσμου καὶ τὰ ἐξουθενημένα ἐξελέξατο ὁ θεός, ⸀τὰ μὴ ὄντα, ἵνα τὰ ὄντα καταργήσῃ,
verses.mistral: And the things that are not, God chose to use, so that the things that are might be brought to nothing.
verses.kjv: And base things of the world, and things which are despised, hath God chosen, yea, and things which are not, to bring to nought things that are:

words.gloss: I bring to naught, sever, abolish

verses.greek: ὅπως μὴ καυχήσηται πᾶσα σὰρξ ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: So that no flesh may boast before God.
verses.kjv: That no flesh should glory in his presence.

words.gloss: before the face of, in the presence of

verses.greek: ἐξ αὐτοῦ δὲ ὑμεῖς ἐστε ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ, ὃς ἐγενήθη ⸂σοφία ἡμῖν⸃ ἀπὸ θεοῦ, δικαιοσύνη τε καὶ ἁγιασμὸς καὶ ἀπολύτρωσις,
verses.mistral: From Him you are in Christ Jesus, who became for us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification and redemption.
verses.kjv: But of him are ye in Christ Jesus, who of God is made unto us wisdom, and righteousness, and sanctification, and redemption:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἵνα καθὼς γέγραπται· Ὁ καυχώμενος ἐν κυρίῳ καυχάσθω.
verses.mistral: Who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.
verses.kjv: That, according as it is written, He that glorieth, let him glory in the Lord.

verses.greek: Κἀγὼ ἐλθὼν πρὸς ὑμᾶς, ἀδελφοί, ἦλθον οὐ καθ’ ὑπεροχὴν λόγου ἢ σοφίας καταγγέλλων ὑμῖν τὸ ⸀μαρτύριον τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: I came to you, brethren, not in lofty words or wisdom, proclaiming to you the testimony of God.
verses.kjv: And I, brethren, when I came to you, came not with excellency of speech or of wisdom, declaring unto you the testimony of God.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ἔκρινά ⸂τι εἰδέναι⸃ ἐν ὑμῖν εἰ μὴ Ἰησοῦν Χριστὸν καὶ τοῦτον ἐσταυρωμένον·
verses.mistral: Not that I know of among you except Jesus Christ, and Him crucified.
verses.kjv: For I determined not to know any thing among you, save Jesus Christ, and him crucified.

verses.greek: κἀγὼ ἐν ἀσθενείᾳ καὶ ἐν φόβῳ καὶ ἐν τρόμῳ πολλῷ ἐγενόμην πρὸς ὑμᾶς,
verses.mistral: I became among you in weakness, in fear, and in much trembling.
verses.kjv: And I was with you in weakness, and in fear, and in much trembling.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ λόγος μου καὶ τὸ κήρυγμά μου οὐκ ἐν ⸂πειθοῖ σοφίας⸃ ἀλλ’ ἐν ἀποδείξει πνεύματος καὶ δυνάμεως,
verses.mistral: My word and my preaching were not in persuasive wisdom but in demonstration of the Spirit and power.
verses.kjv: And my speech and my preaching was not with enticing words of man's wisdom, but in demonstration of the Spirit and of power:

verses.greek: ἵνα ἡ πίστις ὑμῶν μὴ ᾖ ἐν σοφίᾳ ἀνθρώπων ἀλλ’ ἐν δυνάμει θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Faith among you should not be in human wisdom, but in the power of God.
verses.kjv: That your faith should not stand in the wisdom of men, but in the power of God.

verses.greek: Σοφίαν δὲ λαλοῦμεν ἐν τοῖς τελείοις, σοφίαν δὲ οὐ τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου οὐδὲ τῶν ἀρχόντων τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου τῶν καταργουμένων·
verses.mistral: We speak wisdom among those who are mature, but not the wisdom of this age or of the rulers of this age, who are passing away.
verses.kjv: Howbeit we speak wisdom among them that are perfect: yet not the wisdom of this world, nor of the princes of this world, that come to nought:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
I bring to naught, sever, abolish

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ λαλοῦμεν ⸂θεοῦ σοφίαν⸃ ἐν μυστηρίῳ, τὴν ἀποκεκρυμμένην, ἣν προώρισεν ὁ θεὸς πρὸ τῶν αἰώνων εἰς δόξαν ἡμῶν·
verses.mistral: We speak the wisdom of God in a mystery, the hidden wisdom which God predestined before the ages for our glory.
verses.kjv: But we speak the wisdom of God in a mystery, even the hidden wisdom, which God ordained before the world unto our glory:

verses.greek: ἣν οὐδεὶς τῶν ἀρχόντων τοῦ αἰῶνος τούτου ἔγνωκεν, εἰ γὰρ ἔγνωσαν, οὐκ ἂν τὸν κύριον τῆς δόξης ἐσταύρωσαν·
verses.mistral: Which no rulers of this age knew, for if they had known, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.
verses.kjv: Which none of the princes of this world knew: for had they known it, they would not have crucified the Lord of glory.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
I come to know, learn, realize
an untranslatable word that makes a statement contingent

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ καθὼς γέγραπται· Ἃ ὀφθαλμὸς οὐκ εἶδεν καὶ οὖς οὐκ ἤκουσεν καὶ ἐπὶ καρδίαν ἀνθρώπου οὐκ ἀνέβη, ⸀ὅσα ἡτοίμασεν ὁ θεὸς τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: What no eye has seen, nor ear heard, nor the heart of man imagined, what God has prepared for those who love him.
verses.kjv: But as it is written, Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath prepared for them that love him.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἡμῖν ⸀γὰρ ⸂ἀπεκάλυψεν ὁ θεὸς⸃ διὰ τοῦ ⸀πνεύματος, τὸ γὰρ πνεῦμα πάντα ἐραυνᾷ, καὶ τὰ βάθη τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: God has revealed to us through the Spirit; for the Spirit searches all things, even the depths of God.
verses.kjv: But God hath revealed them unto us by his Spirit: for the Spirit searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God.

words.gloss: I uncover, bring to light, reveal

verses.greek: τίς γὰρ οἶδεν ἀνθρώπων τὰ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου εἰ μὴ τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ ἀνθρώπου τὸ ἐν αὐτῷ; οὕτως καὶ τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ οὐδεὶς ⸀ἔγνωκεν εἰ μὴ τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: No human knows what is of a man except the spirit of a man. So also no one has known what is of God except the Spirit of God.
verses.kjv: For what man knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God knoweth no man, but the Spirit of God.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: ἡμεῖς δὲ οὐ τὸ πνεῦμα τοῦ κόσμου ἐλάβομεν ἀλλὰ τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ, ἵνα εἰδῶμεν τὰ ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ χαρισθέντα ἡμῖν·
verses.mistral: We have not received the spirit of the world but the Spirit who is from God, so that we may understand what God has freely given us.
verses.kjv: Now we have received, not the spirit of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἃ καὶ λαλοῦμεν οὐκ ἐν διδακτοῖς ἀνθρωπίνης σοφίας λόγοις, ἀλλ’ ἐν διδακτοῖς ⸀πνεύματος, πνευματικοῖς πνευματικὰ συγκρίνοντες.
verses.mistral: We are not carrying on a debate in the mere intellectual discourse of human wisdom, but we are carrying it on in a discernment given by the Spirit, using spiritual thoughts to evaluate spiritual things.
verses.kjv: Which things also we speak, not in the words which man's wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost teacheth; comparing spiritual things with spiritual.

verses.greek: Ψυχικὸς δὲ ἄνθρωπος οὐ δέχεται τὰ τοῦ πνεύματος τοῦ θεοῦ, μωρία γὰρ αὐτῷ ἐστίν, καὶ οὐ δύναται γνῶναι, ὅτι πνευματικῶς ἀνακρίνεται·
verses.mistral: Natural man does not receive the things of the Spirit of God, for they are foolishness to him; neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned.
verses.kjv: But the natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God: for they are foolishness unto him: neither can he know them, because they are spiritually discerned.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ πνευματικὸς ἀνακρίνει ⸀τὰ πάντα, αὐτὸς δὲ ὑπ’ οὐδενὸς ἀνακρίνεται.
verses.mistral: The spiritual man judges all things, but he himself is judged by no one.
verses.kjv: But he that is spiritual judgeth all things, yet he himself is judged of no man.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τίς γὰρ ἔγνω νοῦν κυρίου, ὃς συμβιβάσει αὐτόν; ἡμεῖς δὲ νοῦν Χριστοῦ ἔχομεν.
verses.mistral: Who knows the mind of the Lord, that he may become his? We have the mind of Christ.
verses.kjv: For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may instruct him? But we have the mind of Christ.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Κἀγώ, ἀδελφοί, οὐκ ἠδυνήθην ⸂λαλῆσαι ὑμῖν⸃ ὡς πνευματικοῖς ἀλλ’ ὡς ⸀σαρκίνοις, ὡς νηπίοις ἐν Χριστῷ.
verses.mistral: I, brethren, could not speak to you as spiritual people, but as mere infants in Christ.
verses.kjv: And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ.

verses.greek: γάλα ὑμᾶς ἐπότισα, ⸀οὐ βρῶμα, οὔπω γὰρ ἐδύνασθε. ἀλλ’ ⸀οὐδὲ ἔτι νῦν δύνασθε,
verses.mistral: I have fed you with milk, not solid food; for up to now you cannot handle it. Yet, even now, you are not able.
verses.kjv: I have fed you with milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able.

words.gloss: I cause to drink, give to drink

verses.greek: ἔτι γὰρ σαρκικοί ἐστε. ὅπου γὰρ ἐν ὑμῖν ζῆλος καὶ ⸀ἔρις, οὐχὶ σαρκικοί ἐστε καὶ κατὰ ἄνθρωπον περιπατεῖτε;
verses.mistral: You are still carnal. Where there is jealousy and strife, are you not acting in a human manner?
verses.kjv: For ye are yet carnal: for whereas there is among you envying, and strife, and divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men?

words.gloss: by no means, not at all
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ὅταν γὰρ λέγῃ τις· Ἐγὼ μέν εἰμι Παύλου, ἕτερος δέ· Ἐγὼ Ἀπολλῶ, ⸂οὐκ ἄνθρωποί⸃ ἐστε;
verses.mistral: Are ye not carnal, when one saith, I am of Paul, and another, I am of Apollos?
verses.kjv: For while one saith, I am of Paul; and another, I am of Apollos; are ye not carnal?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.mistral: In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when you are gathered together and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: παραδοῦναι τὸν τοιοῦτον τῷ Σατανᾷ εἰς ὄλεθρον τῆς σαρκός, ἵνα τὸ πνεῦμα σωθῇ ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τοῦ ⸀κυρίου.
verses.mistral: Deliver such a person to Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that his spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord.
verses.kjv: To deliver such an one unto Satan for the destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the day of the Lord Jesus.

verses.greek: Οὐ καλὸν τὸ καύχημα ὑμῶν. οὐκ οἴδατε ὅτι μικρὰ ζύμη ὅλον τὸ φύραμα ζυμοῖ;

verses.kjv: Your glorying is not good. Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump?

verses.greek: ἐκκαθάρατε τὴν παλαιὰν ζύμην, ἵνα ἦτε νέον φύραμα, καθώς ἐστε ἄζυμοι. καὶ γὰρ τὸ πάσχα ⸀ἡμῶν ἐτύθη Χριστός·
verses.mistral: Clean out the old leaven, so that you may be a new lump, as you are unleavened. Indeed, our Passover lamb has been sacrificed in Christ.
verses.kjv: Purge out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, as ye are unleavened. For even Christ our passover is sacrificed for us:

verses.greek: ὥστε ἑορτάζωμεν, μὴ ἐν ζύμῃ παλαιᾷ μηδὲ ἐν ζύμῃ κακίας καὶ πονηρίας, ἀλλ’ ἐν ἀζύμοις εἰλικρινείας καὶ ἀληθείας.
verses.mistral: Celebrate, not with old leaven nor with the leaven of malice and wickedness, but with unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.
verses.kjv: Therefore let us keep the feast, not with old leaven, neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness; but with the unleavened bread of sincerity and truth.

verses.mistral: Who then is my reward? That I, while preaching the gospel, may not be made a slave to it—constrained to preach the gospel—so that I might become free to be beyond its control in my own authority within the gospel. However, for your requested direct translation without additional context: Who then is my reward? So that in preaching the gospel I may not be made a slave, constrained to preach, but rather be beyond control in the gospel by my own authority.
verses.kjv: What is my reward then? Verily that, when I preach the gospel, I may make the gospel of Christ without charge, that I abuse not my power in the gospel.

verses.mistral: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helps, administrations, various tongues.
verses.kjv: And God hath set some in the church, first apostles, secondarily prophets, thirdly teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, diversities of tongues.

verses.greek: μὴ πάντες ἀπόστολοι; μὴ πάντες προφῆται; μὴ πάντες διδάσκαλοι; μὴ πάντες δυνάμεις;
verses.mistral: Not all are apostles, not all are prophets, not all are teachers, not all are miracles.
verses.kjv: Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?

verses.greek: μὴ πάντες χαρίσματα ἔχουσιν ἰαμάτων; μὴ πάντες γλώσσαις λαλοῦσιν; μὴ πάντες διερμηνεύουσιν;
verses.mistral: Not all have gifts of healing. Not all speak in tongues. Not all interpret.
verses.kjv: Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?

verses.greek: ζηλοῦτε δὲ τὰ χαρίσματα τὰ ⸀μείζονα. καὶ ἔτι καθ’ ὑπερβολὴν ὁδὸν ὑμῖν δείκνυμι.
verses.mistral: Desire earnestly the better gifts. And yet I show you by revelation.
verses.kjv: But covet earnestly the best gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more excellent way.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: Ἐὰν ταῖς γλώσσαις τῶν ἀνθρώπων λαλῶ καὶ τῶν ἀγγέλων, ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω, γέγονα χαλκὸς ἠχῶν ἢ κύμβαλον ἀλαλάζον.
verses.mistral: If I speak in human and angelic tongues and have not love, I am only a resounding gong or a clashing cymbal.
verses.kjv: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born
I make a sound, sound when struck
I cry aloud, a clanging or clashing cymbal

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἐὰν⸃ ἔχω προφητείαν καὶ εἰδῶ τὰ μυστήρια πάντα καὶ πᾶσαν τὴν γνῶσιν, ⸄καὶ ἐὰν⸅ ἔχω πᾶσαν τὴν πίστιν ὥστε ὄρη ⸀μεθιστάναι, ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω, οὐθέν εἰμι.
verses.mistral: And if I have prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge, even if I have faith so as to move mountains, but do not have love, I am nothing.
verses.kjv: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.

words.gloss: I move out of its place, transfer
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἐὰν⸃ ψωμίσω πάντα τὰ ὑπάρχοντά μου, ⸄καὶ ἐὰν⸃ παραδῶ τὸ σῶμά μου, ἵνα ⸀καυθήσομαι, ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω, οὐδὲν ὠφελοῦμαι.
verses.mistral: If I give away all that I have, and deliver up my body to be burned, but do not have love, I am of no advantage.
verses.kjv: And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.

words.gloss: I am, exist, am in possession
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη μακροθυμεῖ, χρηστεύεται ἡ ἀγάπη, οὐ ζηλοῖ ⸂ἡ ἀγάπη⸃, οὐ περπερεύεται, οὐ φυσιοῦται,
verses.mistral: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud.
verses.kjv: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,

words.gloss: I suffer long, have patience, am forbearing

verses.greek: οὐκ ἀσχημονεῖ, οὐ ζητεῖ τὰ ἑαυτῆς, οὐ παροξύνεται, οὐ λογίζεται τὸ κακόν,
verses.mistral: Does not act unbe becoming, does not seek its own, is not easily angered, does not keep a record of wrongs.
verses.kjv: Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;

verses.greek: οὐ χαίρει ἐπὶ τῇ ἀδικίᾳ, συγχαίρει δὲ τῇ ἀληθείᾳ·
verses.mistral: Rejoices not in wrongdoing, but rejoices in truth.
verses.kjv: Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth;

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: πάντα στέγει, πάντα πιστεύει, πάντα ἐλπίζει, πάντα ὑπομένει.
verses.mistral: All covers, all believes, all hopes, all endures.
verses.kjv: Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.

verses.greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη οὐδέποτε ⸀πίπτει. εἴτε δὲ προφητεῖαι, καταργηθήσονται· εἴτε γλῶσσαι, παύσονται· εἴτε γνῶσις, καταργηθήσεται.
verses.mistral: Love never fails. Even if prophecies cease, or tongues speak, or knowledge increases.
verses.kjv: Charity never faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I bring to naught, sever, abolish
I bring to naught, sever, abolish

verses.greek: ἐκ μέρους ⸀γὰρ γινώσκομεν καὶ ἐκ μέρους προφητεύομεν·
verses.mistral: Partially, for we know in part and prophesy in part
verses.kjv: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ ἔλθῃ τὸ τέλειον, ⸀τὸ ἐκ μέρους καταργηθήσεται.
verses.mistral: When the complete comes, what is partial will be done away with.
verses.kjv: But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I bring to naught, sever, abolish

verses.greek: ὅτε ἤμην νήπιος, ⸂ἐλάλουν ὡς νήπιος, ἐφρόνουν ὡς νήπιος, ἐλογιζόμην ὡς νήπιος⸃· ⸀ὅτε γέγονα ἀνήρ, κατήργηκα τὰ τοῦ νηπίου.
verses.mistral: When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I thought as a child, I reasoned as a child; when I became a man, I put away childish things.
verses.kjv: When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
I bring to naught, sever, abolish

verses.greek: βλέπομεν γὰρ ἄρτι δι’ ἐσόπτρου ἐν αἰνίγματι, τότε δὲ πρόσωπον πρὸς πρόσωπον· ἄρτι γινώσκω ἐκ μέρους, τότε δὲ ἐπιγνώσομαι καθὼς καὶ ἐπεγνώσθην.
verses.mistral: Now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I shall know as I am fully known.
verses.kjv: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, learn, realize
but, on the other hand, and
I come to know, discern, recognize
I come to know, discern, recognize

verses.greek: νυνὶ δὲ μένει πίστις, ἐλπίς, ἀγάπη· τὰ τρία ταῦτα, μείζων δὲ τούτων ἡ ἀγάπη.
verses.mistral: Now remains faith, hope, and love; but the greatest of these is love.
verses.kjv: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Διώκετε τὴν ἀγάπην, ζηλοῦτε δὲ τὰ πνευματικά, μᾶλλον δὲ ἵνα προφητεύητε.
verses.mistral: Pursue love, yet strive for spiritual gifts, but especially that you may prophesy.
verses.kjv: Follow after charity, and desire spiritual gifts, but rather that ye may prophesy.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ λαλῶν γλώσσῃ οὐκ ἀνθρώποις λαλεῖ ⸀ἀλλὰ θεῷ, οὐδεὶς γὰρ ἀκούει, πνεύματι δὲ λαλεῖ μυστήρια·
verses.mistral: Speaks in a tongue does not speak to people but to God; no one understands, but by the Spirit he speaks mysteries.
verses.kjv: For he that speaketh in an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ προφητεύων ἀνθρώποις λαλεῖ οἰκοδομὴν καὶ παράκλησιν καὶ παραμυθίαν.
verses.mistral: Builds up, encourages, and comforts people (the one prophesying)
verses.kjv: But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men to edification, and exhortation, and comfort.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὁ λαλῶν γλώσσῃ ἑαυτὸν οἰκοδομεῖ· ὁ δὲ προφητεύων ἐκκλησίαν οἰκοδομεῖ.
verses.mistral: Speaking in tongues builds up the self; but prophesying builds up the church.
verses.kjv: He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: θέλω δὲ πάντας ὑμᾶς λαλεῖν γλώσσαις, μᾶλλον δὲ ἵνα προφητεύητε· μείζων ⸀δὲ ὁ προφητεύων ἢ ὁ λαλῶν γλώσσαις, ἐκτὸς εἰ μὴ ⸀διερμηνεύῃ, ἵνα ἡ ἐκκλησία οἰκοδομὴν λάβῃ.
verses.mistral: Wish all of you to speak in tongues, but rather that you prophesy. He who prophesies is greater than he who speaks in tongues, except he interprets, so that the church may be built up.
verses.kjv: I would that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye prophesied: for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸀Νῦν δέ, ἀδελφοί, ἐὰν ἔλθω πρὸς ὑμᾶς γλώσσαις λαλῶν, τί ὑμᾶς ὠφελήσω, ἐὰν μὴ ὑμῖν λαλήσω ἢ ἐν ἀποκαλύψει ἢ ἐν γνώσει ἢ ἐν προφητείᾳ ἢ ἐν διδαχῇ;
verses.mistral: If I come to you speaking in tongues, what good will I be to you, unless I speak to you in a revelation or a knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
verses.kjv: Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, except I shall speak to you either by revelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ὅμως τὰ ἄψυχα φωνὴν διδόντα, εἴτε αὐλὸς εἴτε κιθάρα, ἐὰν διαστολὴν τοῖς φθόγγοις μὴ ⸀δῷ, πῶς γνωσθήσεται τὸ αὐλούμενον ἢ τὸ κιθαριζόμενον;
verses.mistral: Although I give you melodious sounds, whether it is a harp or a lyre, if there is no distinction in the tones, how will the one playing the harp be known, or the one playing the lyre?
verses.kjv: And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: καὶ γὰρ ἐὰν ἄδηλον ⸂φωνὴν σάλπιγξ⸃ δῷ, τίς παρασκευάσεται εἰς πόλεμον;
verses.mistral: And if the trumpet gives an unclear sound, who will prepare for battle?
verses.kjv: For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς διὰ τῆς γλώσσης ἐὰν μὴ εὔσημον λόγον δῶτε, πῶς γνωσθήσεται τὸ λαλούμενον; ἔσεσθε γὰρ εἰς ἀέρα λαλοῦντες.
verses.mistral: So also you, unless you speak the right words, how will it be known what is being said? For you shall be speaking into the air.
verses.kjv: So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: τοσαῦτα εἰ τύχοι γένη φωνῶν ⸀εἰσιν ἐν κόσμῳ, καὶ ⸀οὐδὲν ἄφωνον·
verses.mistral: Varied are the sounds in the world, and nothing is without sound.
verses.kjv: There are, it may be, so many kinds of voices in the world, and none of them is without signification.

verses.greek: ἐὰν οὖν μὴ εἰδῶ τὴν δύναμιν τῆς φωνῆς, ἔσομαι τῷ λαλοῦντι βάρβαρος καὶ ὁ λαλῶν ἐν ἐμοὶ βάρβαρος.
verses.mistral: If I do not know the power of the voice, I shall be a foreigner to him who speaks, and he who speaks will be a foreigner to me.
verses.kjv: Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me.

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς, ἐπεὶ ζηλωταί ἐστε πνευμάτων, πρὸς τὴν οἰκοδομὴν τῆς ἐκκλησίας ζητεῖτε ἵνα περισσεύητε.
verses.mistral: So also you, since you are eager for spiritual gifts, seek to abound in the building up of the church.
verses.kjv: Even so ye, forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual gifts, seek that ye may excel to the edifying of the church.

verses.greek: ⸀Διὸ ὁ λαλῶν γλώσσῃ προσευχέσθω ἵνα διερμηνεύῃ.
verses.mistral: Let him who speaks in a tongue pray that he may interpret.
verses.kjv: Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.

verses.greek: ἐὰν γὰρ προσεύχωμαι γλώσσῃ, τὸ πνεῦμά μου προσεύχεται, ὁ δὲ νοῦς μου ἄκαρπός ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: If I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my mind is unfruitful.
verses.kjv: For if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my understanding is unfruitful.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τί οὖν ἐστιν; προσεύξομαι τῷ πνεύματι, προσεύξομαι δὲ καὶ τῷ νοΐ· ψαλῶ τῷ πνεύματι, ψαλῶ δὲ καὶ τῷ νοΐ·
verses.mistral: What therefore shall it be? If I pray to the spirit, I pray also to the mind. I sing with the spirit, I sing also with the mind.
verses.kjv: What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐπεὶ ἐὰν ⸀εὐλογῇς ⸀πνεύματι, ὁ ἀναπληρῶν τὸν τόπον τοῦ ἰδιώτου πῶς ἐρεῖ τὸ Ἀμήν ἐπὶ τῇ σῇ εὐχαριστίᾳ; ἐπειδὴ τί λέγεις οὐκ οἶδεν·
verses.mistral: If you bless with the spirit, who fills the place of the one, how will he say "Amen" on your thanksgiving? For what are you saying that he does not know?
verses.kjv: Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?

words.gloss: I fill up, make up, fulfill
verily, truly, amen, so let it be
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: σὺ μὲν γὰρ καλῶς εὐχαριστεῖς, ἀλλ’ ὁ ἕτερος οὐκ οἰκοδομεῖται.
verses.mistral: You thank well, but the other is not edified.
verses.kjv: For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.

verses.greek: εὐχαριστῶ τῷ ⸀θεῷ, πάντων ὑμῶν μᾶλλον ⸀γλώσσαις ⸀λαλῶ·
verses.mistral: I thank God more boldly through you all, speaking in various languages.
verses.kjv: I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ θέλω πέντε λόγους ⸂τῷ νοΐ⸃ μου λαλῆσαι, ἵνα καὶ ἄλλους κατηχήσω, ἢ μυρίους λόγους ἐν γλώσσῃ.
verses.mistral: In the church, I wish to speak five words with my mind, so that I may also instruct others, or many words in a language.
verses.kjv: Yet in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding, that by my voice I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue.

verses.greek: Ἀδελφοί, μὴ παιδία γίνεσθε ταῖς φρεσίν, ἀλλὰ τῇ κακίᾳ νηπιάζετε, ταῖς δὲ φρεσὶν τέλειοι γίνεσθε.
verses.mistral: Brothers, be not children in your minds, yet in wickedness be babes; but in understanding be mature.
verses.kjv: Brethren, be not children in understanding: howbeit in malice be ye children, but in understanding be men.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἐν τῷ νόμῳ γέγραπται ὅτι Ἐν ἑτερογλώσσοις καὶ ἐν χείλεσιν ⸀ἑτέρων λαλήσω τῷ λαῷ τούτῳ, καὶ οὐδ’ οὕτως εἰσακούσονταί μου, λέγει κύριος.
verses.mistral: In the law it is written, "In foreign tongues and in the lips of foreigners I will speak to this people, and even so they will not listen to Me," says the Lord.
verses.kjv: In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not hear me, saith the Lord.

verses.greek: ὥστε αἱ γλῶσσαι εἰς σημεῖόν εἰσιν οὐ τοῖς πιστεύουσιν ἀλλὰ τοῖς ἀπίστοις, ἡ δὲ προφητεία οὐ τοῖς ἀπίστοις ἀλλὰ τοῖς πιστεύουσιν.
verses.mistral: The languages serve as a sign, not for those who believe, but for those who do not believe. However, prophecy is not for unbelievers, but for believers.
verses.kjv: Wherefore tongues are for a sign, not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but prophesying serveth not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐὰν οὖν συνέλθῃ ἡ ἐκκλησία ὅλη ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ καὶ πάντες ⸂λαλῶσιν γλώσσαις⸃, εἰσέλθωσιν δὲ ἰδιῶται ἢ ἄπιστοι, οὐκ ἐροῦσιν ὅτι μαίνεσθε;
verses.mistral: If the entire church comes together and all speak in tongues, yet outsiders or unbelievers enter, they will not say that you are mad.
verses.kjv: If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those that are unlearned, or unbelievers, will they not say that ye are mad?

words.gloss: I come or go with, accompany, assemble
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I go in, come in, enter
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ πάντες προφητεύωσιν, εἰσέλθῃ δέ τις ἄπιστος ἢ ἰδιώτης, ἐλέγχεται ὑπὸ πάντων, ἀνακρίνεται ὑπὸ πάντων,
verses.mistral: If all prophesy, and an unbeliever or outsider enters, he is convicted by all, examined by all.
verses.kjv: But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or one unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I go in, come in, enter
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ⸀τὰ κρυπτὰ τῆς καρδίας αὐτοῦ φανερὰ γίνεται, καὶ οὕτως πεσὼν ἐπὶ πρόσωπον προσκυνήσει τῷ θεῷ, ἀπαγγέλλων ὅτι ⸂Ὄντως ὁ θεὸς⸃ ἐν ὑμῖν ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: The hidden things of his heart are made manifest, and so falling on his face he will worship God, announcing that indeed God is among you.
verses.kjv: And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I report, bring a report, announce

verses.greek: Τί οὖν ἐστιν, ἀδελφοί; ὅταν συνέρχησθε, ⸀ἕκαστος ψαλμὸν ἔχει, διδαχὴν ἔχει, ⸂ἀποκάλυψιν ἔχει, γλῶσσαν⸃ ἔχει, ἑρμηνείαν ἔχει· πάντα πρὸς οἰκοδομὴν γινέσθω.
verses.mistral: What therefore is it, brethren? When you come together, each one has a hymn, a teaching, a revelation, a tongue, a interpretation. Let all things be done for building up.
verses.kjv: How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto edifying.

words.gloss: I come or go with, accompany, assemble
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: εἴτε γλώσσῃ τις λαλεῖ, κατὰ δύο ἢ τὸ πλεῖστον τρεῖς, καὶ ἀνὰ μέρος, καὶ εἷς διερμηνευέτω·
verses.mistral: One therefore who speaks in a tongue let it be by two or at the most three, and in turn, and one interprets.
verses.kjv: If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
and, apiece, by, each, every, in, through

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ᾖ ⸀διερμηνευτής, σιγάτω ἐν ἐκκλησίᾳ, ἑαυτῷ δὲ λαλείτω καὶ τῷ θεῷ.
verses.mistral: If there is no interpreter, let him keep silent in the church, and let him speak to himself and to God.
verses.kjv: But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: προφῆται δὲ δύο ἢ τρεῖς λαλείτωσαν, καὶ οἱ ἄλλοι διακρινέτωσαν·
verses.mistral: Two or three prophets should speak, and the others should evaluate.
verses.kjv: Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ ἄλλῳ ἀποκαλυφθῇ καθημένῳ, ὁ πρῶτος σιγάτω.
verses.mistral: If another is revealed as prophesying, let the first be silent.
verses.kjv: If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I uncover, bring to light, reveal

verses.greek: δύνασθε γὰρ καθ’ ἕνα πάντες προφητεύειν, ἵνα πάντες μανθάνωσιν καὶ πάντες παρακαλῶνται

verses.kjv: Howbeit that was not first which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.

verses.greek: ὁ πρῶτος ἄνθρωπος ἐκ γῆς χοϊκός, ὁ δεύτερος ⸀ἄνθρωπος ἐξ οὐρανοῦ.
verses.mistral: The first man is from earth, the second man is from heaven.
verses.kjv: The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.

verses.greek: οἷος ὁ χοϊκός, τοιοῦτοι καὶ οἱ χοϊκοί, καὶ οἷος ὁ ἐπουράνιος, τοιοῦτοι καὶ οἱ ἐπουράνιοι·
verses.mistral: Like the earthly, so also are the earthly; and like the heavenly, so also are the heavenly.
verses.kjv: As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy: and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.

verses.greek: καὶ καθὼς ἐφορέσαμεν τὴν εἰκόνα τοῦ χοϊκοῦ, ⸀φορέσομεν καὶ τὴν εἰκόνα τοῦ ἐπουρανίου.
verses.mistral: And so we shall bear the image of the heavenly.
verses.kjv: And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.

verses.greek: Τοῦτο δέ φημι, ἀδελφοί, ὅτι σὰρξ καὶ αἷμα βασιλείαν θεοῦ κληρονομῆσαι οὐ ⸀δύναται, οὐδὲ ἡ φθορὰ τὴν ἀφθαρσίαν κληρονομεῖ.
verses.mistral: This is my saying, brethren: Flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does corruption inherit immortality.
verses.kjv: Now this I say, brethren, that flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth corruption inherit incorruption.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἰδοὺ μυστήριον ὑμῖν λέγω· ⸀πάντες οὐ κοιμηθησόμεθα πάντες δὲ ἀλλαγησόμεθα,
verses.mistral: All shall not sleep, but we shall all be changed.
verses.kjv: Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἐν ἀτόμῳ, ἐν ῥιπῇ ὀφθαλμοῦ, ἐν τῇ ἐσχάτῃ σάλπιγγι· σαλπίσει γάρ, καὶ οἱ νεκροὶ ἐγερθήσονται ἄφθαρτοι, καὶ ἡμεῖς ἀλλαγησόμεθα.
verses.mistral: In an instant, in the blink of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we shall be changed.
verses.kjv: In a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the trumpet shall sound, and the dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be changed.

verses.greek: δεῖ γὰρ τὸ φθαρτὸν τοῦτο ἐνδύσασθαι ἀφθαρσίαν καὶ τὸ θνητὸν τοῦτο ἐνδύσασθαι ἀθανασίαν.
verses.mistral: This must be clothed with imperishability, and this mortal must be clothed with immortality.
verses.kjv: For this corruptible must put on incorruption, and this mortal must put on immortality.

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ τὸ ⸂φθαρτὸν τοῦτο ἐνδύσηται ἀφθαρσίαν καὶ τὸ⸃ θνητὸν τοῦτο ἐνδύσηται ⸀ἀθανασίαν, τότε γενήσεται ὁ λόγος ὁ γεγραμμένος· Κατεπόθη ὁ θάνατος εἰς νῖκος.
verses.mistral: Then shall be clothed the perishable with imperishability, and the mortal with immortality. Then shall come to pass the written word: Death has been swallowed up in victory.
verses.kjv: So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Death is swallowed up in victory.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ποῦ σου, θάνατε, τὸ ⸂νῖκος; ποῦ σου, θάνατε, τὸ κέντρον⸃;
verses.mistral: Where, O Death, is your victory? Where, O Death, is your sting?
verses.kjv: O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ κέντρον τοῦ θανάτου ἡ ἁμαρτία, ἡ δὲ δύναμις τῆς ἁμαρτίας ὁ νόμος·
verses.mistral: The core of death is sin, but the power of sin is the law.
verses.kjv: The sting of death is sin; and the strength of sin is the law.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: τῷ δὲ θεῷ χάρις τῷ διδόντι ἡμῖν τὸ νῖκος διὰ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: To the God be grace, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: But thanks be to God, which giveth us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ὥστε, ἀδελφοί μου ἀγαπητοί, ἑδραῖοι γίνεσθε, ἀμετακίνητοι, περισσεύοντες ἐν τῷ ἔργῳ τοῦ κυρίου πάντοτε, εἰδότες ὅτι ὁ κόπος ὑμῶν οὐκ ἔστιν κενὸς ἐν κυρίῳ.
verses.mistral: Stand firm, beloved brothers, be steadfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that your labor is not in vain in the Lord.
verses.kjv: Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye stedfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Περὶ δὲ τῆς λογείας τῆς εἰς τοὺς ἁγίους, ὥσπερ διέταξα ταῖς ἐκκλησίαις τῆς Γαλατίας, οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ποιήσατε.
verses.mistral: According to the collection for the saints, just as I instructed the churches of Galatia, so do in the same way.
verses.kjv: CORINTHIANS

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: κατὰ μίαν ⸀σαββάτου ἕκαστος ὑμῶν παρ’ ἑαυτῷ τιθέτω θησαυρίζων ὅ τι ⸀ἐὰν εὐοδῶται, ἵνα μὴ ὅταν ἔλθω τότε λογεῖαι γίνωνται.
verses.mistral: Each of you should set aside and save, if he wishes to give, on the first day of every week. So that when I come, no collections shall be made.
verses.kjv: Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
I store up, treasure up, save
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ παραγένωμαι, οὓς ⸀ἐὰν δοκιμάσητε δι’ ἐπιστολῶν, τούτους πέμψω ἀπενεγκεῖν τὴν χάριν ὑμῶν εἰς Ἰερουσαλήμ·
verses.mistral: When I come, I will send those whom you approved by letters to carry your gift to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I put to the test, prove, examine

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ ⸂ἄξιον ᾖ⸃ τοῦ κἀμὲ πορεύεσθαι, σὺν ἐμοὶ πορεύσονται.
verses.mistral: If it is necessary for me to go also, they will go with me.
verses.kjv: And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Ἐλεύσομαι δὲ πρὸς ὑμᾶς ὅταν Μακεδονίαν διέλθω, Μακεδονίαν γὰρ διέρχομαι,
verses.mistral: I will come to you when I pass through Macedonia, for I am passing through Macedonia.
verses.kjv: Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I pass through, spread a report
I pass through, spread a report

verses.greek: πρὸς ὑμᾶς δὲ τυχὸν ⸀παραμενῶ ἢ ⸀καὶ παραχειμάσω, ἵνα ὑμεῖς με προπέμψητε οὗ ἐὰν πορεύωμαι.
verses.mistral: Meet you then, and stay with you or even winter with you, so that you may send me on my journey as I journey.
verses.kjv: And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: οὐ θέλω γὰρ ὑμᾶς ἄρτι ἐν παρόδῳ ἰδεῖν, ἐλπίζω ⸀γὰρ χρόνον τινὰ ἐπιμεῖναι πρὸς ὑμᾶς, ἐὰν ὁ κύριος ⸀ἐπιτρέψῃ.
verses.mistral: I do not wish now to see you in my journey, I hope for a while to remain with you, if the Lord permits.
verses.kjv: For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.

verses.greek: ⸀ἐπιμενῶ δὲ ἐν Ἐφέσῳ ἕως τῆς πεντηκοστῆς·
verses.mistral: Remain in Ephesus until the Pentecost.
verses.kjv: But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: θύρα γάρ μοι ἀνέῳγεν μεγάλη καὶ ἐνεργής, καὶ ἀντικείμενοι πολλοί.
verses.mistral: Great and powerful door has been opened to me, and many adversaries.
verses.kjv: For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.

verses.greek: Ἐὰν δὲ ἔλθῃ Τιμόθεος, βλέπετε ἵνα ἀφόβως γένηται πρὸς ὑμᾶς, τὸ γὰρ ἔργον κυρίου ἐργάζεται ὡς ⸀κἀγώ·
verses.mistral: If Timothy comes, see that he may be bold among you, for the work of the Lord he is doing as I am.
verses.kjv: Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I come into being, am born

verses.greek: μή τις οὖν αὐτὸν ἐξουθενήσῃ. προπέμψατε δὲ αὐτὸν ἐν εἰρήνῃ, ἵνα ἔλθῃ πρός ⸀με, ἐκδέχομαι γὰρ αὐτὸν μετὰ τῶν ἀδελφῶν.
verses.mistral: Do not despise him. Send him on his way in peace, so that he may come to me; for I expect him along with the brethren.
verses.kjv: Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: Περὶ δὲ Ἀπολλῶ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ, πολλὰ παρεκάλεσα αὐτὸν ἵνα ἔλθῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς μετὰ τῶν ἀδελφῶν· καὶ πάντως οὐκ ἦν θέλημα ἵνα νῦν ἔλθῃ, ἐλεύσεται δὲ ὅταν εὐκαιρήσῃ.
verses.mistral: Regarding Apollos, my fellow worker, I strongly urged him to come to you with the brothers; but he was not willing to come now, he will come when it is convenient for him.
verses.kjv: As touching our brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
but, on the other hand, and
I have a good opportunity, have leisure

verses.greek: Γρηγορεῖτε, στήκετε ἐν τῇ πίστει, ἀνδρίζεσθε, κραταιοῦσθε.
verses.mistral: Stay awake, stand firm in the faith, be courageous, be strong.
verses.kjv: Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.

words.gloss: I am awake, am vigilant, watch
I act like a man, am brave

verses.greek: πάντα ὑμῶν ἐν ἀγάπῃ γινέσθω.
verses.mistral: All of you be in love.
verses.kjv: Let all your things be done with charity.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς, ἀδελφοί· οἴδατε τὴν οἰκίαν Στεφανᾶ, ὅτι ἐστὶν ἀπαρχὴ τῆς Ἀχαΐας καὶ εἰς διακονίαν τοῖς ἁγίοις ἔταξαν ἑαυτούς·
verses.mistral: I exhort you, brethren, regarding the house of Stephanas, that you know it is the firstfruits of Achaia and they have devoted themselves to serving the saints.
verses.kjv: I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: ἵνα καὶ ὑμεῖς ὑποτάσσησθε τοῖς τοιούτοις καὶ παντὶ τῷ συνεργοῦντι καὶ κοπιῶντι.
verses.mistral: Obey also those who labor among you, and are over you in the Lord and admonish you.
verses.kjv: That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.

verses.greek: χαίρω δὲ ἐπὶ τῇ παρουσίᾳ Στεφανᾶ καὶ Φορτουνάτου καὶ Ἀχαϊκοῦ, ὅτι τὸ ⸀ὑμέτερον ὑστέρημα οὗτοι ἀνεπλήρωσαν,
verses.mistral: I am pleased upon the coming of Stephen, Fortunatus, and Achaius, that they have fulfilled your deficiency.
verses.kjv: I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
on, to, against, on the basis of, at
I fill up, make up, fulfill

verses.greek: ἀνέπαυσαν γὰρ τὸ ἐμὸν πνεῦμα καὶ τὸ ὑμῶν. ἐπιγινώσκετε οὖν τοὺς τοιούτους.
verses.mistral: Your pneuma (spirit) has not ceased in me, and it is now apparent to you who are such.
verses.kjv: For they have refreshed my spirit and your's: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.

words.gloss: I make to rest, give rest to, rest, take my ease
I come to know, discern, recognize

verses.greek: Ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς αἱ ἐκκλησίαι τῆς Ἀσίας. ⸀ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς ἐν κυρίῳ πολλὰ Ἀκύλας καὶ ⸀Πρίσκα σὺν τῇ κατ’ οἶκον αὐτῶν ἐκκλησίᾳ.
verses.mistral: The churches in Asia greet you. A lot of Aquila and Priscilla greet you, together with the church that is in their house.
verses.kjv: The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to
I greet, salute, pay my respects to
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ἀσπάζονται ὑμᾶς οἱ ἀδελφοὶ πάντες. ἀσπάσασθε ἀλλήλους ἐν φιλήματι ἁγίῳ.
verses.mistral: All the brethren greet you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
verses.kjv: All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to
I greet, salute, pay my respects to

verses.greek: Ὁ ἀσπασμὸς τῇ ἐμῇ χειρὶ Παύλου.
verses.mistral: Paul's greeting to you.
verses.kjv: The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.

verses.greek: εἴ τις οὐ φιλεῖ τὸν ⸀κύριον, ἤτω ἀνάθεμα. ⸂Μαράνα θά⸃.
verses.mistral: If anyone does not love the Lord, let him be accursed. The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you. Amen. Marana tha.
verses.kjv: If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha.

verses.greek: ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου ⸀Ἰησοῦ μεθ’ ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with you.
verses.kjv: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

verses.greek: ἡ ἀγάπη μου μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἐν Χριστῷ ⸀Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
